data.title: SMX announces effective date of reverse stock split
data.description:
• SMX (Security Matters) Public Limited Company (SMX) announced on Thursday that the reverse stock split of the company's ordinary shares will begin trading on an adjusted basis, giving effect to the reverse stock split on February 17, 2026, under the existing ticker symbol "SMX."
  • The new CUSIP number of the company's ordinary shares will be G8267K406, and the new ISIN code will be IE000B5COQZ5.
  • The company's board of directors fixed the split ratio at 4.8828125:1; every 4.8828125 ordinary shares of the company with a nominal value of $0.00000000002502543568 per share will be automatically combined into one ordinary share with a nominal value of $0.00000000012219451015625 per share.
  • The company said that this will reduce the number of outstanding ordinary shares of the Company from approximately 10 million to approximately 2 million.
  • Outstanding company options, warrants, and other applicable convertible securities, including the company's warrants listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol SMXWW, which will retain its existing CUSIP number, will be proportionately adjusted in accordance with their respective terms, it added. 
  • SMX is -14.82% to $14.25.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nebius falls after Q4 revenue miss; plans new data center in France
data.description:
Shares of Nebius (NBIS) fell about 3% on Thursday after its fourth quarter revenue missed estimates.

The Dutch AI infrastructure provider saw revenue for the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31 surge 547% year-over-year to $227.7M. However, the numbers came in below the consensus estimate of $242.79M.

The company's fourth quarter adjusted net loss widened to -$173M compared to -$69M in the year ago period.

The company noted that it ended the year with about 170 MW of active power versus its target of 100 MW.

"We enter 2026 with strong momentum, high operational confidence, and a scalable platform built for the world’s most demanding AI workloads. We are on track to end the year with ARR1 of $7 billion to $9 billion. To achieve this we will focus on four key strategic areas: scaling capacity with discipline; executing on our customer commitments; advancing our AI Cloud platform; and raising capital strategically," said Founder and CEO Arkady Volozh in a letter to shareholders.

Annual Recurring Revenue, or ARR, as of year-end was $1.25B, ahead of the company's most recent guidance of $900M to $1.1B. During 2025, the company significantly diversified its AI cloud customer portfolio by adding several large startup and enterprise logos, according to Volozh.

Volozh noted that in the fourth quarter of 2025, capital expenditures were about $2.1B, mainly driven by purchases of GPUs and GPU-related hardware and its data center expansion activities, including investments in planned greenfield sites. 

The company noted that it remains on track to reach 800 MW to 1 GW of connected power by the end of 2026. The company now expects to have more than 3 GW of contracted power by year-end. 

Nebius is planning a new 240 MW data center in Béthune, France, that will be one of Europe’s largest when it is finished, Reuters reported.

Nebius did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Last month, Nebius (NBIS) and its competitors, Supermicro (SMCI) and CoreWeave (CRWV), said they will start offering Nvidia's new Vera Rubin NVL72 computing platform.

Supermicro dipped about 1%, while CoreWeave was largely flat on Thursday.

Update: The story was updated with stock price movement and capital expenditure figures.

data.title: ‘SaaS apocalypse is entirely unwarranted’ – Constellation Research’s Ray Wang
data.description:
The current selloff in software stocks (IGPT), (XSW), (IGV) is an overreaction driven by misplaced fears that artificial intelligence will eliminate the need for software, according to Ray Wang, founder and chairman of Constellation Research.

In an interview with CNBC, Wang argued that high-performing companies are being unfairly punished by investors despite strong fundamentals, calling the situation a “SaaS apocalypse” that is entirely unwarranted.

“The way they’re being undervalued makes no sense at this moment because I think people are overreacting saying that software is going to go away with AI, but not all software is going away with AI,” Wang said.

He pointed to ServiceNow (NOW) and Salesforce (CRM) as examples of companies caught in the downturn despite impressive performance metrics, noting that ServiceNow (NOW) is operating at “rule 50, probably going to rule 60” levels.

Wang used Cisco Systems (CSCO) as a prime example of a company being punished by Wall Street despite being well-positioned to capture significant AI spending. The company has $2.1B in hyperscaler AI infrastructure orders and just released a new Silicon One G300 chip, yet investors reacted negatively to its guidance.

“The Street’s in an anti-AI mode, and everyone’s dumping AI for this moment in time and taking their gains from the beginning of the year,” Wang explained.

The analyst emphasized that Cisco (CSCO) is squarely positioned to benefit from an estimated $700B in capital expenditure spending among major tech companies, placing it alongside NVIDIA (NVDA) and TSMC (TSM) as primary beneficiaries of AI infrastructure investment.

Wang noted that the new G300 chip, a three-nanometer switch chip from TSMC, introduces much-needed competition to a market currently dominated by Broadcom (AVGO) and NVIDIA (NVDA).

Looking ahead, Wang predicted that investors will rotate back into technology stocks (ARTY), (AIQ), (XLK), (VGT) by the second quarter when they struggle to find growth opportunities elsewhere.

“When they have nothing to invest in, where there’s nothing growing in the 30% range, they’re probably going to come back and say, we’ve got to get back into tech,” he said.

Wang also expressed support for Bill Ackman’s recent $2B stake in Meta (META), citing the company’s 24% year-over-year growth in ad revenue and its dominant position in the consumer market.

data.title: Check Point falls after mixed Q4, announces 3 acquisitions done in Q1
data.description:
Shares of Check Point Software Technologies (CHKP) fell about 4% premarket on Thursday after mixed fourth-quarter results.

The cybersecurity company also announced three acquisitions carried out in the first quarter of 2026 of Cyata, Cyclops, and Rotate.

Q4 Metrics

Revenue for the fourth quarter, ended Dec. 31, rose about 6% year-over-year to $745M, missing estimates. Meanwhile, non-GAAP EPS surged 26% year-over-year to $3.40, beating analysts' expectations.

Remaining Performance Obligation, or RPO, was about $2.73B, an 8% increase year over year.

"We delivered solid fourth quarter and full year 2025 results, with revenue landing above the midpoint of our outlook and EPS exceeding expectations. Our performance remained resilient throughout the year, driven by continued customer adoption across our Hybrid Mesh Network and Workspace platforms," said CEO Nadav Zafrir.

Revenue from Products and Licenses rose 0.7% year-over-year to $171.8M, while Security Subscriptions revenues climbed 11% year-on-year to $325.1M.

Calculated Billings reached about $1.04B, an 8% increase year over year.

Outlook

"In 2026, our strategy is centered on securing our customers’ AI transformation across the enterprise. We are focused on executing against our four strategic pillars, Hybrid Mesh, Workspace, and Exposure Management, while embedding AI driven security throughout our portfolio. Today’s announced acquisition of Cyata further expands our AI security stack, enabling full discovery, governance, and control of AI agents as organizations accelerate their AI journeys," said Zafrir.

The company expects first quarter 2026 EPS between $2.35 and $2.45 (midpoint at $2.40), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $2.37.

Check Point forecasts revenue between $655M and $685M (midpoint at $670M) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $677.45M.

The company expects full year 2026 EPS between $10.05 and $10.85 (midpoint at $10.45), compared to a consensus EPS estimate of $10.48.

Check Point expects full year revenue between $2.85 and 2.95B (midpoint at $2.9B) versus a consensus revenue estimate of $2.90B.

Acquisitions

Check Point said it acquired Cyata to help accelerate its mission to deliver an end-to-end AI security platform for the agentic world.

The company acquired Cyclops to expand its Exposure Management offering to deliver a complete Continuous Threat Exposure Management, or CTEM, solution to the market.

Check Point added that it acquired Rotate — an all-in-one platform purpose-built for managed service providers, or MSPs. The company noted that it acquired the talent of Rotate to accelerate the strong Workspace momentum in the MSP market.

Update: The story was updated with data on the company's outlook.

data.title: Most shorted large-cap stocks in January
data.description:
A cluster of consumer, AI, and crypto-linked names dominates the latest list of the most shorted large-cap stocks as of January, with short interest topping 25% across the board and exceeding 30% for several companies.

Topping the list is Under Armour (UAA) (UA), which saw its short interest (as a % of shares outstanding) fall slightly from 34.96% at December-end to 33.41% at January 30. Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) moved up three places to take the second spot, with 32.95% short interest, while CleanSpark (CLSK) retained its third spot with 32.23% short interest.

Here’s a list of the top 10 most shorted mid- to mega-cap stocks (as a % of shares outstanding) as of January 30:

  • Under Armour (UAA) – 33.41%
  • Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – 32.95%
  • CleanSpark (CLSK) – 32.23%
  • Recursion Pharmaceuticals (RXRX) – 31.77%
  • Applied Digital (APLD) – 30.85%
  • SoundHound AI (SOUN) – 30.53%
  • Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) – 28.24%
  • MARA Holdings (MARA) – 27.82%
  • Sphere Entertainment (SPHR) – 25.32%
  • Centrus Energy (LEU) – 25.29%

data.title: AppLovin drops over competitor concerns, but analysts highlight positive growth drivers
data.description:
Despite posting stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter results and guidance, AppLovin (APP) shares had dropped 13% during early market trading on Thursday, likely due to concerns over artificial intelligence and rising competition from Meta (META), Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) and CloudX in the in-game ad environment.

However, analysts remained mostly positive on the stock as it has growth drivers ahead, such as its e-commerce self-serve platform.

"On META, the company emphasized that Meta Audience Network has been a longtime bidder within APP's ad supply and that incremental bidding activity on IDFA traffic is not new," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Matthew Cost, in an investor note. "Importantly, they also argued the competitive landscape today is fundamentally different than five years ago (when META was last dominant) given APP's performance gains and the closed-loop data advantage the company has built."

Morgan Stanley retained its Overweight rating but reduced its price target to $720 from $800.

"Our main focus remains on the ecommerce ramp through 1H:26 and into the general launch of the web self-serve tool," Cost added. "With APP now trading at 17x our '27 EBITDA, a breakthrough in ecommerce/web ads would likely be an important driver of multiple expansion and earnings revisions for '26 and beyond."

Meanwhile, Jefferies retained its Buy rating and reduced its price target to $700 from $860. The financial firm highlighted that gaming-exposed names continue to sell off as the "AI risk is being applied indiscriminately."

"Self‑serve GA remains on track for 1H this year, though APP is taking a deliberate approach to scaling e‑comm rather than rushing deployment," said Jefferies analysts, led by James Heaney, in a note. "While new advertisers are showing solid 30‑day LTV/CAC payback, only 57% of qualified leads are converting, leaving room to optimize the conversion funnel. A key bottleneck is insufficient video creative, which limits advertisers' ability to ramp effectively. APP plans to launch GenAI creative tools in the coming months, which mgmt believes could eventually lift conversion closer to 70–80%, at which point a broader GA launch becomes more compelling."

AppLovin CEO and co-founder Adam Foroughi directly addressed the competition narrative during the earnings call.

"As you know from our business over the last couple of years that you've been following, there have been numerous times where we've added more competition in the MAX auction," he said. "Unity (U) has grown with Vector, Liftoff rolled out Cortex, and it's growing really quickly. Moloco has been growing, and we turned them into a bidder in the last year. You had Google switch to bidding. Every time this has happened, there's been this confusion around, well, in an auction dynamic-based system, competition should decay AppLovin's edge. You've never seen that happen."

"The reason is that, one, we're really big and really good at what we do," he added. "But two, every impression is not worth the same thing. Our model is exceptionally good at valuing the impression for what it is."

AppLovin competitor Unity was down 8% in early market trading after closing 26% lower on Wednesday.

data.title: South Korea’s KOSPI outpaces global peers, even as concentration tops the S&P 500
data.description:
South Korea’s benchmark KOSPI Composite Index (KOSPI) has surged 31% year-to-date, vaulting above the 5,000 level and emerging as one of the strongest-performing major equity markets around the world to start the year.

The rally underscores renewed investor enthusiasm for the country’s technology sector and improving global demand trends. 

However, the advance has been highly concentrated. Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (HXSC.F) together account for roughly 46% of the KOSPI’s total market capitalization, meaning nearly half of the index’s gains have been driven by just two semiconductor heavyweights. Their outsized influence highlights the index’s significant exposure to the global chip cycle and artificial intelligence-related demand. 

The concentration dynamic stands out even when compared with the U.S. market. The Magnificent Seven — Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META), and Tesla (TSLA) — collectively make up about 33% of the S&P 500 (SP500). While investors have voiced concerns about narrow leadership in U.S. equities, the KOSPI’s structure suggests an even more top-heavy composition. 

For investors seeking exposure to South Korean equities, the iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) offers a widely used vehicle that tracks the country’s large- and mid-cap stocks, including its dominant technology names. 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX). 

Mag 7 Focused ETFs: (MAGS), (MAGX), (QQQU), and (QQQD).

data.title: Freddie Mac Q4 earnings slip as net worth tops $70B
data.description:
Freddie Mac (FMCC) stock gained 1.5% in Thursday premarket trading 

The government-sponsored enterprise that, along with Fannie Mae (FNMA), backs a majority of U.S. mortgages, saw its net worth rise to $70.4B, up 4.1% Q/Q and 18% Y/Y.

"Freddie Mac strengthened its financial position in 2025, earning net income of $10.7B on revenue of $23.3B and increasing its net worth to $70.4B," said CEO Kenny Smith.

Q4 comprehensive income of $2.78B vs. $2.79B in Q3 and $3.19B in Q4 2024.

Q4 net revenue of $5.76B increased from $5.74B in the prior quarter and declined from $6.33B in the year-ago period. Net interest income grew/dropped to $5.55B from $5.46B in Q3 and $5.05B in Q4 2024, while noninterest income of $217 dipped from $284M in the previous quarter and $1.28B in the year-ago quarter.

Its provision for credit losses of $52M compared with $175M in Q3 and $92M in the prior year’s Q4.

In Q4, its single-family segment new business activity increased to $118B in unpaid principal balance ((UPB)) from $99B in the prior quarter and $100B a year ago. The Y/Y increase was driven by an increase in refinance activity.

Its single-family mortgage portfolio rose to $3.16T from $3.14T in Q3. The serious delinquency rate ticked up to 0.59% from 0.57% in the previous quarter and 0.59% in the year-ago period.

The multifamily segment’s new business activity climbed to $29B in Q4 from $25B in Q3 and edged down from $30B in the prior year’s Q4. Its mortgage portfolio grew to $496B UPB from $480B in the prior quarter. And its delinquency rate fell to 0.44% from 0.51% in Q3 and 0.40% in Q4 2024.

Q4 noninterest expense of $2.26B increased from $2.12B in Q3 and $2.22B in Q4 2024.

data.title: Alibaba's AI app Qwen sees over 120M orders in holiday campaign
data.description:
Alibaba (BABA) said its AI app Qwen has generated over 120M orders from consumers over the past six days, from Feb. 6 to Feb. 11.

Alibaba said in a blog post that since Feb. 6, it has upgraded the Qwen App by integrating Freshippo — the company's new retail platform for groceries and fresh goods — its business-to-consumer online grocery arm Tmall Supermarket, and entertainment ticketing platform Damai. This move allows consumers to order food and beverages, book flights, and buy movie tickets all through the Qwen app while enjoying significant incentives during the Chinese New Year holiday.

Qwen App has ranked number one on the Apple App Store’s free apps chart in China for the past six consecutive days, according to the company.

Alibaba is offering a free shopping voucher worth 25 yuan (about $3.60) to every user of its Qwen app, which enables consumers to buy food, drinks, and groceries online. In addition, the Qwen app is offering digital red envelopes containing random amounts of money to users, allowing them to win up to 2,888 yuan in cash, The Information reported.

The company intends to spend around 3B yuan (about $431M) in total on Qwen’s overall holiday giveaway campaign.

Competitors ByteDance (BDNCE) and Tencent (TCEHY) (TCTZF) have all started offering giveaways and other incentives to promote their AI chatbot apps ahead of the Lunar New Year this month. ByteDance and Tencent have unveiled similar campaigns with red envelopes and other promotions.
